def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    # Calculate momentum indicators
    rsi = ta.momentum.RSIIndicator(df['close'], window=params['rsi_window']).rsi()
    stoch = ta.momentum.StochasticOscillator(
        high=df['high'],
        low=df['low'],
        close=df['close'],
        window=params['stoch_window'],
        smooth_window=params['stoch_smooth']
    ).stoch()
    
    # Calculate price change threshold
    price_change = df['close'].pct_change(params['momentum_window'])
    
    # Generate signals
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    
    # Long condition: RSI oversold AND stochastic oversold AND positive momentum
    signals[(rsi < params['rsi_low']) & 
            (stoch < params['stoch_low']) & 
            (price_change > params['momentum_threshold'])] = 1
    
    # Short condition: RSI overbought AND stochastic overbought AND negative momentum
    signals[(rsi > params['rsi_high']) & 
            (stoch > params['stoch_high']) & 
            (price_change < -params['momentum_threshold'])] = -1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: EUR/GBP exhibits momentum reversals when overbought/oversold conditions confirmed by both RSI and Stochastic indicators coincide with significant short-term price momentum.
